layers.title: Context routes sampler design
layers.summary: The repository context now routes logging-sampler questions to a new design document. That design establishes the request, access, and diagnostic sampling model that the implementation layers follow.
layers.diff:
+| design unified class-based random log sampling | `modules/infra/logging/log-sampler-design.md` | `src/datasystem/common/log/*`, `src/datasystem/common/log/spdlog/*`, `src/datasystem/common/metrics/hard_disk_exporter/*`, `src/datasystem/common/util/gflag/*` |
+- If the question is about unified class-based random log sampling:
+  - read `log-sampler-design.md`
++ `LogSampler` provides unified random hash-threshold sampling across request/access/diagnostic dimensions, with per-request granularity and low-overhead hot-path decisions.
++## Purpose
++- What problem this module solves:
++  - sample interface logs and request logs before expensive payload formatting, while preserving complete logs for
++    sampled-in requests and allowing diagnostic-only retention for non-sampled-in requests.

layers.title: Operator docs replace limit
layers.summary: The user-facing documentation moves from per-second `log_rate_limit` to fractional sampling rates. It also removes the standalone client environment variable from the documented surface.
layers.diff:
-| 25 | `DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT` | `0` | 每秒采样请求数上限（0表示不限速）。仅对带 traceId 的请求日志生效：被采样到的请求会完整打印链路日志，未采样请求的链路日志会被整体丢弃（包含 WARNING/ERROR/FATAL）。 |
-大流量场景下，可通过 `log_rate_limit` 参数控制每秒采样请求数，避免高频请求日志产生过多磁盘 I/O。
+LogSampler 提供统一随机哈希阈值采样，替代旧的 `log_rate_limit` first-N 限速机制。
+- 三类独立采样率：`request_sample_rate`、`access_sample_rate`、`diagnostic_sample_rate`（各 0.0–1.0，默认 1.0=全量保留）
-| log_rate_limit | int | `0` | 每秒采样请求数上限（0表示不限速）。仅对带 traceId 的请求日志生效：被采样到的请求会完整打印链路日志，未采样请求的链路日志会被整体丢弃（包含 WARNING/ERROR/FATAL）。 |
+| request_sample_rate | double | `1.0` | 请求日志采样率（0.0–1.0）。1.0=全量保留；0.0=丢弃请求级 INFO/VLOG，请求级 ERROR/WARNING/PLOG 由 diagnostic_sample_rate 控制 |

bcFindings.evidence: docs/source_zh_cn/appendix/client_env_guide.md deletes the documented DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT environment variable with no replacement environment variable.

layers.title: Embedded API changes
layers.summary: The public embedded-worker configuration method is replaced by three double-valued sampling-rate methods. This is the first consumer-visible layer because callers must compile against the new method names and value model.

layers.title: Flags validate atomically
layers.summary: The gflag layer declares and validates the three new sample-rate flags. Dynamic updates now commit sampler-related flags as a batch, so partial invalid updates do not publish a half-new sampler configuration.

layers.title: Deployment knobs renamed
layers.summary: The deployment defaults and generated worker configs stop emitting `log_rate_limit`. They now expose empty or templated sample-rate fields so request-only derivation can distinguish unspecified values from explicit `1.0`.

bcFindings.evidence: include/datasystem/utils/embedded_config.h removes EmbeddedConfig &LogRateLimit(int limit) and adds RequestSampleRate/AccessSampleRate/DiagnosticSampleRate instead.
src/datasystem/common/util/gflag/flags.h removes log_rate_limit from the handled flag list while config files replace it with request_sample_rate/access_sample_rate/diagnostic_sample_rate.

cohorts.cohort: Sampler core, wire protocol, and propagation

layers.title: Sampling config protobuf
layers.summary: The wire schema grows `LogSampleConfigPb` and carries it on register and heartbeat responses. This is foundational because client-side sampler state is now driven by worker-published ppm values instead of a scalar rate limit.

layers.title: Sampler replaces limiter
layers.summary: The core logging dependency shifts from the deleted first-N `LogRateLimiter` to `LogSampler`. The new sampler keeps a relaxed atomic enabled check on hot paths and publishes immutable snapshots for request, access, and diagnostic rates.

layers.title: Runtime log gates
layers.summary: The spdlog message path now records whether the sampler already checked a message and can skip construction/flush work when sampled out. Trace no longer eagerly creates limiter decisions; request decisions are made lazily by the sampler or RPC propagation path.

layers.title: RPC propagates decisions
layers.summary: The ZMQ metadata helper now asks `LogSampler` to create or apply request sample state. Worker register and heartbeat responses publish the effective `LogSampleConfigPb`, and client-side common code applies the worker-provided sampler config.

bcFindings.evidence: src/datasystem/common/log/spdlog/log_rate_limiter.h and .cpp are deleted, removing the LogRateLimiter class and its public methods.
src/datasystem/protos/share_memory.proto changes field 24 from int32 log_rate_limit to LogSampleConfigPb log_sample_config.

layers.title: Recorder gains facades
layers.summary: AccessRecorder is expanded with object, stream, and outbound-request facades that store references or providers and check sampling before materializing fields. This layer is what makes sampled-out access logs avoid RequestParam construction and string formatting.

layers.title: SDK calls defer payloads
layers.summary: C, C++, Java, and Python binding call sites switch from eagerly building RequestParam/data-size strings to fluent access recorders. Relative to the facade layer, these edits are the broad migration that moves work behind sampler checks on client hot paths.

layers.title: Worker call sites align
layers.summary: Worker object-cache and stream-cache service paths adopt the same lazy access-recording pattern. The worker-side migration keeps access-log formatting semantics centralized while reducing foreground request work when access sampling drops the record.

bcFindings.evidence: access-recorder call sites replace the old Record overload taking string data sizes with facade Record() and uint64_t DataSize(), and the PR description states the old string dataSize signature was removed.

layers.title: Unit coverage pivots
layers.summary: The old rate-limiter unit target is deleted and replaced with sampler, access, config, integration, logging, and RPC metadata coverage. The tests exercise the same categories as the new production sampler instead of the removed per-second quota behavior.

layers.title: Performance scenarios expand
layers.summary: The performance tests are rewritten around random sampling rates, payload construction counts, and access-recorder sampled-in/out latency. This layer verifies the hot-path goal that sampled-out access logs avoid payload building and formatting work.

layers.title: System tests propagate config
layers.summary: A new system test starts a two-worker cluster with all three sampling rates set to 0.5. It checks cross-process sampling consistency and verifies worker-to-client `LogSampleConfigPb` delivery through register/heartbeat paths.

summary: This PR replaces the old per-second log rate limiter with a unified random LogSampler spanning request, access, and diagnostic logs, including worker-to-client config propagation, lazy access-log materialization, updated deployment/docs, and expanded tests.
